ideation: This animation sequence will depict a wolf's predatory stalking behavior.  The robot will mimic the careful, controlled movements of a wolf as it approaches its prey. We will use a combination of subtle arm movements and slight shifts in body weight (implied through arm positioning) to create a sense of tension and anticipation. The sequence will focus primarily on the right arm, simulating a wolf's forward paw position as it moves stealthily. The left arm will provide a counterbalance, subtly adjusting to maintain the robot's stability and overall sense of predatory intent. Frame 1 will show the robot in a neutral stance, setting the stage for the hunt. Frame 2 will have the right arm extend forward, mimicking the careful placement of a wolf's paw. Frame 3 will subtly shift the robot's weight by positioning the left arm slightly backward, enhancing the forward momentum implied by the right arm. Frame 4 will have the right arm lower slightly, preparing for a potential pounce, and the left arm shifts again for balance. Frame 5 will return to a neutral stance, suggesting the wolf is ready to strike or has chosen to observe further.

frames.beat: robot crouches low, ready to stalk
the right arm extends forward, a paw reaching
shifting weight, the left arm pulls back slightly
the right arm lowers, preparing to spring
both arms return to a neutral, watchful pose
